title: ranunculus bulbosus var. valdepubens
intro: Ranunculus bulbosus var. valdepubens, also known as Buttercup, is a perennial plant species in the Ranunculaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers with black centers and glossy, dark green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus bulbosus var. valdepubens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and creams for topical application.
